title: Angsty in Ankara
gist: Foreign Policy's Situation Report captures a NATO alliance still managing Trump ahead of the Ankara summit — including a public Truth Social broadside against European burden-sharing and a stark warning from a former NATO deputy commander that 'America under Trump cannot be trusted' — alongside the abrupt reversal of a U.S. export ban on Anthropic's frontier models and an estimate that the Iran war has already cost each American household $1,000.

insights.claim: Trump renewed his long-standing complaint that the U.S. subsidizes NATO without benefit, posting on Truth Social that 'the United States spends more money on NATO than any other country, by far, to protect them, without getting any benefit from so doing,' just days before he was due to attend the Ankara summit.
insights.speaker: Rishi Iyengar
insights.so_what: A fresh, dated instance of Trump's recurring NATO-funding grievance heading into a major summit — a concrete input for gauging burden-sharing negotiation risk at Ankara.
insights.quote: The United States spends more money on NATO than any other country, by far, to protect them, without getting any benefit from so doing
insights.locator: Nothing is certain in this world besides death, taxes

insights.claim: At last year's Hague summit, NATO allies agreed to raise defense spending to 5% of GDP by 2035; Secretary-General Mark Rutte says the Ankara summit will show 'tens of billions of dollars of new contracts' delivering on that pledge, even as the Iran war has severely depleted U.S. weapons stockpiles Washington itself may need.
insights.speaker: Rishi Iyengar
insights.so_what: Sets a concrete number (5% of GDP by 2035, tens of billions in new contracts) to track NATO defense-spend follow-through — directly relevant to defense-sector order flow and depleted U.S. stockpile risk.
insights.quote: we are going to show the world that we are delivering on the commitments we made in The Hague last year
insights.locator: However, Rutte sought to paint a picture of progress

insights.claim: Retired NATO Deputy Supreme Allied Commander Europe Gen. Richard Shirreff warns that Rutte's approach amounts to 'a continuation of the head-in-the-sand approach of the NATO secretary-general about the reality that America under Trump cannot be trusted as a NATO ally' — a blunt dissent from a former top NATO commander just ahead of the summit.
insights.speaker: Rishi Iyengar
insights.so_what: A credible insider source explicitly flags U.S. reliability risk to NATO — relevant to any European defense-autonomy or defense-spending thesis that assumes reduced confidence in U.S. security guarantees.
insights.quote: a continuation of the head-in-the-sand approach of the NATO secretary-general about the reality that America under Trump cannot be trusted as a NATO ally.
insights.locator: My expectation is that it will be more of the same

insights.claim: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth is pushing Congress to pass Trump's proposed $1.5 trillion FY2027 defense budget, writing that 'the single greatest threat to America's national security today is under-investment in military spending' — a request framed against stockpiles already strained by the Iran war.
insights.speaker: Rishi Iyengar
insights.so_what: A specific, trackable budget figure ($1.5T FY2027) and stated rationale — a leading indicator for U.S. defense-sector spending and appropriations fights to watch.
insights.quote: The single greatest threat to America’s national security today is under-investment in military spending
insights.locator: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth wrote in an op-ed

insights.claim: The Trump administration abruptly reversed, after 18 days, its export-control ban that had forced Anthropic to bar foreign nationals from its Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models entirely; Anthropic said it updated the models' safeguards and committed to 'deeper government collaboration' on future releases, but the initial cutoff had already spooked European allies whose trust may be harder to rebuild.
insights.speaker: Rishi Iyengar
insights.so_what: Flags AI export-control policy volatility itself as a geopolitical/customer-trust risk factor for frontier AI vendors' international business, independent of the underlying technology restrictions.
insights.locator: Fable restored. Eighteen days after the Trump administration

insights.claim: The latest round of indirect U.S.-Iran talks in Doha focused only on Strait of Hormuz navigation and unfreezing Iranian financial assets, notably omitting any discussion of restricting Iran's nuclear program — one of Trump's original stated goals for launching the war — suggesting the post-war memorandum of understanding's initial outcomes favor Iran over Washington.
insights.speaker: Rishi Iyengar
insights.so_what: Signals the Iran deal's substantive terms may be tilting toward Tehran's interests on the two issues (Hormuz access, frozen assets) most relevant to oil-shipping and sanctions-exposure risk, while the headline nuclear-restriction goal has stalled.
insights.locator: Doha doldrums. The United States and Iran concluded

insights.claim: Moody's Analytics chief economist Mark Zandi estimates the Iran war has already cost each American household $1,000, calling his own estimate conservative and noting the true cost is likely meaningfully higher.
insights.speaker: Rishi Iyengar
insights.so_what: A concrete, quotable household-cost figure for gauging the domestic economic drag of the Iran conflict — a useful input for consumer-spending analysis tied to the war.

insights.claim: The U.S.-brokered Israel-Hezbollah cease-fire in Lebanon is already fraying: Israel pledged to withdraw from two areas of southern Lebanon it controls but has delayed that withdrawal, citing Hezbollah's continued use of low-cost drones in the region.
insights.speaker: Rishi Iyengar
insights.so_what: Flags an active ceasefire-compliance risk in a second Middle East front beyond Iran that could reignite regional conflict — relevant to broader Middle East geopolitical-risk monitoring.
insights.locator: The United States last week also brokered a cease-fire

insights.claim: The Pentagon created a new 'Direct Reporting Portfolio Manager for Unmanned Systems' position reporting directly to the deputy secretary of defense to oversee all U.S. military drone programs — a structural signal of institutional prioritization of autonomous/drone warfare.
insights.speaker: Rishi Iyengar
insights.so_what: A concrete organizational change worth tracking for defense-tech and drone-sector procurement, since new dedicated oversight roles often precede budget/program consolidation.
insights.locator: The Defense Department is looking for someone

insights.claim: The Trump administration named Avi Loeb — former head of Harvard's astronomy department, known for questionable theories about UFO sightings — to lead a new government team studying national-security threats from potential alien spacecraft.
insights.speaker: Rishi Iyengar
insights.so_what: A soft signal of how fringe figures are gaining official national-security roles under the administration — low direct market relevance but useful color on institutional risk appetite and credibility.
